title: ITR filing 2026: Are credit card rewards, cashback taxable in India? What you need to know
summary: As ITR filing season nears, credit card rewards in India are generally treated as discounts, not taxable income. However, high-value rewards or those linked to business expenses may attract scrutiny. Here's what experts have to say about its tax treatment.

title: Sales machine or advisory model? RBI’s reset for bank-led insurance
summary: RBI’s draft rules seek to curb mis-selling in the bancassurance model through suitability checks and tighter oversight. Experts say sales may not fall, but scrutiny will rise.

title: 8th Pay Commission: BPMS seeks ₹72,000 minimum pay, 4x fitment factor, 6% annual hike; check details
summary: The 8th Pay Commission continues to gain momentum as Bharatiya Pratiraksha Mazdoor Sangh (BPMS) demands a minimum salary of ₹ 72,000, a 4x fitment factor, and a 6% annual hike to align pay with rising income and living standards.

title: Cashless wallets? This brokerage wants most currency notes scrapped in 5 years
summary: A global brokerage firm urges Prime Minister Narendra Modi to formalize transactions and reduce the use of high-value currency to enhance transparency. Here's what they suggested.

title: Your lunch could save you ₹1 lakh a year, but are you claiming it?
summary: The Income-Tax Rules, 2026 raise the per-meal exemption to ₹200 and extend it to the new regime—turning employer-provided meals into a ₹1.05 lakh annual tax-free benefit.

title: PNB, Kiwi partner to launch Credit-on-UPI Card for 180 million users
summary: Credit on UPI gets a boost as Punjab National Bank partners with Kiwi to launch a feature-rich credit card with cashback and seamless digital payments.

title: Redeeming airline miles may feel harder than ever — here's why your points won't go far anymore
summary: Travellers are increasingly using air miles to book multiple flights, offsetting rising ticket prices and cancellations. Flexible reward programmes were seeing a surge in demand, but miles no longer stretch as far as they once did. Here's why.

title: New NPS exit rules explained: Retiring soon? How much you can withdraw and when
summary: The National Pension System has updated withdrawal rules, allowing more flexibility for subscribers until 2026. Government employees exiting prematurely must use 80% of their accumulated pension wealth for an annuity, while those with an APW of ₹5 lakh or less can withdraw fully in a lump sum.

title: What made Putin sell 22,000 kg gold from Russia this year?
summary: Russia’s central bank has sold 21.8 tonnes or 22,000 kilograms of gold so far in 2026 to help fund the country’s widening budget deficit, which had reached $61.2 billion by the end of March, a Kitco report said, citing Russian and Ukrainian news.

summary: The Indian rupee fell for the fourth consecutive ​session on Thursday, its ​longest losing streak since mid-January, as stalled peace talks ​between the U.S. and Iran lifted oil prices over $100 per barrel.
